content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as nested ecosystems of micro-agents, where each micro-agent autonomously decides which memory traces to preserve, query for labeling, and prune based on its own local curiosity-driven reward signal. This hierarchical micro-agent framework resolves the longstanding SOTA tradeoff between memory compression and retention of rare high-value information that generic active learning frameworks struggle to address.
